kept.text:
Just crossed $10k earned on Contra😍✨️

Not from one big project.
Not overnight.

but one thing that made the biggest difference?
🔻 Referrals and repeat clients.

A good number of these projects didn’t come from applying to jobs.

They came from past clients coming back or sending someone my way.

That only happens when: 

✅️ You deliver what you promised
✅️ You communicate well
✅️ You make the process easy for the client

Early on, it’s easy to focus only on getting new clients.

But keeping clients and doing work they’re happy to talk about matters just as much.

That’s what compounds.

If you’re freelancing, focus on doing work people remember🤝

Still early. More to build. Going for more✔️

kept.text:
$18k in sales first month, store built in one weekend

someone used claude to create the entire shopify catalog: titles, descriptions, pricing, tags, collections, navigation

50 products structured and uploaded without touching a spreadsheet

defined the logic once, claude executed across everything

traditional way to build a 50-product shopify store:

> hire a VA or content manager
> write 50 product descriptions manually
> research keywords for each listing
> organize collections and tags
> set up navigation and categories
> upload everything one by one
> 2-3 weeks minimum, $500-2000 in labor

his way:

> connected claude to shopify documentation and APIs
> wrote one PRD (product requirement document) defining brand voice, pricing logic, tag structure
> fed raw product data and images
> claude generated everything: titles, descriptions, tags, product types
> auto-assigned products to collections
> organized store structure and navigation

time spent: one weekend

the store launched monday morning

by friday: first $4k in orders

by end of month: $18k total sales

his secret wasn’t the products - it was the system

most people treat claude like a chatbot, asking one question at a time

he treated it like an employee with a playbook

the PRD told claude exactly how to name products, what tone to use, which price tiers to follow, how to structure tags for SEO

claude didn’t guess - it followed the system

when he wanted to add 20 more products next month, same process
feed data, claude executes, store updates

no retraining, no explaining from scratch
the playbook already exists

he’s now testing the same system on amazon merch and etsy digital

same product ideas, adapted for each platform

claude rewrites listings for how buyers search on each marketplace

one idea becomes three revenue streams

the store still runs, he still checks quality and approves listings

but the 80% of repetitive work - writing, organizing, structuring - is gone

article.title: Claude Code Replaced Entire E-Commerce Team. 5 Platforms. $127K/Year
article.preview_text:
How Claude Code is replacing the e-commerce operating system : 
Most e-commerce projects fail not because of the idea, but because of the operational side. You need to research niches, create

kept.text:
do you understand what just happened to the e-commerce soul..

They replaced an entire 5-person team: content manager, designer, assistant, analyst, and dev, with a single AI agent on the morning of a $127,000 budget review.

The Claude Code shift was nuclear: 
→ research, SEO, and copywriting are no longer human roles 
→ one prompt executes a workflow across 5 different platforms (Amazon, Etsy, Shopify, etc.) 
→ the "operational side" that kills most startups has been automated out of existence

Gone.. Today.. While agencies were still arguing about "AI quality."

The $127K/year payroll didn't need to be optimized.
Claude Code proved it was obsolete.

kept.text:
For those new here, I’m Abiodun😊

I design and redesign Shopify websites for eCommerce brands that want more than just a “nice-looking” store.

The focus is simple:

✅️ Clear structure, better user experience, and product pages that actually convert visitors into buyers.

I also share what I’ve learned from freelancing. 

✔️ How I get clients, structure projects, and grow consistently without relying on luck.

Most of my posts are based on real work, real results, and lessons from actual client projects.

If you run an eCommerce brand or you’re building a freelance career, you’ll find something useful here.

Follow along🤝

kept.text:
Shopify released a dedicated "Hermes Agent skill" for Nous Research's open-source AI agent framework.

This native integration enables a Hermes Agent to autonomously manage a Shopify storefront. 

The agent is empowered to handle end-to-end e-commerce operations, including managing products, tracking inventory levels, processing orders, and executing fulfillments across multiple channels.

kept.text:
Most e-commerce projects don't fail because of a bad idea

They fail because one person can't handle research, listings, SEO images, and pricing all at once

Claude Code doesn't replace your judgment
It removes everything that was eating your time

What actually works: Claude Code

Not (ask AI to write a description)

Build repeatable workflows once, then run them everywhere

>> Amazon Merch: upload design>> full listing keywords
>>Etsy Digital: drop a bestseller link>> get gap analysis + improved 
>> Shopify: raw product data >> full catalog, copy

One good listing prompt runs for months
You refine it reuse it, scale it

With Claude everything gets faster
